title: Masala Dosa
description: Masala Dosa is one of South India's most iconic dishes, featuring a crispy fermented rice and lentil crêpe filled with a lightly spiced potato masala. Served with coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and sambar, it is a beloved breakfast and restaurant favorite.

ingredients.title: For the Dosa Batter
items: Raw rice (dosa rice or parboiled idli rice) – 2 cups
Urad dal (split black gram, skinless) – ½ cup

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Dosa Batter
steps: Wash the rice, urad dal, chana dal, and fenugreek seeds thoroughly.
Soak them together in water for about 6 hours.
Soak the poha separately for 30 minutes before grinding.
Grind everything together with cold water into a smooth, slightly thick batter.
Ferment the batter overnight (8–12 hours) in a warm place until doubled in volume.
Add salt and mix gently before making dosas.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Potato Masala
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a pan.
Add mustard seeds and allow them to splutter.
Add chana dal, urad dal, dry red chilies, and curry leaves.
Add green chilies, grated ginger, and sliced onions. Sauté until the onions become translucent.
Mix in turmeric powder and salt.
Add the mashed potatoes and combine well.
Sprinkle a little water for a soft texture and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Finish with chopped coriander leaves.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Masala Dosa
steps: Heat a well-seasoned cast iron tawa or non-stick griddle over medium-high heat.
Lightly grease the surface with oil.
Pour a ladle of batter into the center and spread it outward in a thin circular motion.
Drizzle a few drops of oil or ghee around the edges.
Cook until the dosa becomes crisp and golden brown.
Place 2–3 tablespoons of potato masala in the center.
Fold the dosa into a roll or triangle and serve immediately.

Restaurant Style: Serve immediately while crisp with chutneys and sambar.
Brush the dosa lightly with butter or ghee before serving for extra richness.

chefTips: Maintain a 4:1 ratio of rice to urad dal for crisp and flavorful dosas.
The batter should remain slightly thick; thin it only when making dosas if needed.
Always stir the fermented batter gently without overmixing.
Cook on medium-high heat. A low flame makes the dosa pale, while excessive heat may burn it before it crisps.
Keep the potato masala soft and mildly seasoned so it complements the crispy dosa.
For restaurant-quality results, use a well-seasoned cast iron tawa.
